The Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to effect the immediate transfer of the 20% share of the NTA to the Special Account – 20% Development Fund upon receipt of the NTA to fast tract the implementation of the PPAs to be funded from said funds.

The Municipal Treasurer to proactively forward the list of delinquent taxpayers to the Provincial Taxing authority and coordinate with them the imposition/implementation of the civil remedies in collecting taxes.

Recommendation:

Recommendations:
We recommended and the management agreed to require its responsible officers of the following:
Property Unit to physically segregate and promptly list down all obsolete and unserviceable PPE with complete data in the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP), duly signed by the Municipal Accountant, Inspector and Appraisal Committee, and approved by the Head of the Agency or authorized representative, and submit to the Disposal Committee for disposal as early as possible;
Disposal Committee to immediately dispose of all unserviceable/obsolete PPEs using the appropriate mode of disposal as determined, to avoid further deterioration, earn income from its sale, if warranted, and make use of the storage spaces occupied by these items; and
The GSO to submit the necessary documents together with the Official Receipt after disposal, as the basis to drop the unserviceable properties from the books of accounts to come up with the accurate balance of the PPE account in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular Nos. 89-296 and 92-386.

Ensure the timely implementation of the projects funded by national government agencies.

Recommendations:
We recommended and management agreed the following on each observation:
Incomplete reversion of prior years’ PPAs in the LDRRMF Investment Plan for CY 2023 as well as non-update of the current year’s LDRRMF appropriations.
The LDRRMO to fully reprogram the funds recorded in the Special Trust Fund and include the reprogrammed PPAs in the revised LDRRMFIP; and
The LDRRMO, in the subsequent preparation of LDRRMFIP, to timely coordinate with the Municipal Budget Officer for any changes in the appropriations of each PPAs (reversion/supplemental/realignment) and effect such changes in the Investment Plan to ensure accurate monitoring in the utilization of funds and to avoid over/under utilization of funds in the implementation of its PPAs.
Non-implementation of some reprogrammed PPAs in the prior years’ fund totaling P1.525 million.
The LDRRMO to institute proper planning of the identified PPAs which includes providing doable timelines for its implementation and appropriating reasonable funds based on its desired output;
The LDRRMO to monitor and evaluate the full implementation of the PPAs and the optimal utilization of its funds by providing its status reports periodically so that any surplus of the funds after said PPAs have been fully implemented may be appropriated to another PPAs; and
The LDRRMO to evaluate the unimplemented PPAs in accordance with their attainability and consider the reprogramming of PPAs for the subsequent year that is still responsive to the needs of its constituents and the LGU with respect to disaster prevention, preparedness, response and recovery in the midst of the pandemic, and optimally implement identified PPAs within the prescribed timeline.
Unreconciled appropriation and utilization of prior years’ funds.
The LDRRMO to submit justification on the procurement of food for impounded dogs without an appropriation provided in their revised LDRRMFIP;
The LDRRMO to correctly classify the appropriations in their PPAs as MOOE or Capital Outlay in their LDRRMFIP and furnish a copy to the Municipal Budget Office to ensure correctness in the monitoring on utilization of its budget; and
The LDRRMO and the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Municipal Budget Office in reconciling the appropriation and utilization balances reflected in their respective records to ensure efficient monitoring and accurate reporting in the movement of funds.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations

      We recommend to management the following:   1.)  Prepare  monthly  the
Bank Reconciliation Statements and submit the same within  the  reglementary
period prescribed under COA  Circular  No.  96-011  dated  October  2,  1996
together with its  supporting  documents  such  as  Monthly  Bank  Statement
Snapshot to facilitate the early submission of the BRS, Subsidiary  Ledgers,
paid checks, debit and credit memos and deposit slips; and 2.)  Prepare  the
necessary adjusting entries for any reconciling items noted in the process.

Recommendation

      We recommend the following:  1.) The  Local  Finance  Committee  adopt
appropriate measures to control not only the appropriations, allotments  but
the cash availability  as  well;  2.)  The  Municipal  Budget  Officer  show
details with regard to the computation of unappropriated balances;  and  3.)  The Municipal Treasurer, in certifying that Funds are available  by  signing
Box B of the Disbursement Voucher and preparing check for any  disbursement,
ensure that indeed funds are available  for  the  purpose  to  preclude  the
incurrence of cash deficit.

Recommendations

      We recommend to management the following: 1.) Reclassify the  Advances
to Operating Expenses to the corresponding  proper  account  of  Advance  to
Special  Disbursing  Officer  and  Advances  to  Officers   and   Employees,
respectively;  2.)  refrain  from  granting  additional  cash  advances   to
Accountable Officer with previous unliquidated cash  advances;  3.)  enforce
full liquidation of cash advances within the  reglementary  period  provided
under COA Circular  No.97-002  dated  February  10,  1997;  4.)  demand  the
concerned officials and employees with unliquidated cash advances to  effect
immediate settlement of cash advances,  otherwise,  the  necessary  sanction
for its settlement as provided for in paragraph  9.1  of  COA  Circular  No.
2012-004 dated November 28, 2012 shall be  enforced;  and  comply  with  the
provisions of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 to file  for
request of write-off on dormant accounts.

Recommendation

      We recommend that management:  1.) Create an  inventory  committee  to
conduct a  physical  count  of  the  properties  of  the  municipality;  2.)
Maintain a Property, Plant and Equipment Register to facilitate the  conduct
of year-end physical inventory, the municipality  use this as basis  in  the
conduct and reconciliation of the accounts; and 3.) Submit  a  copy  of  the
RPCPPE to the Office of the Auditor not later  than  January  31st  of  each
year as provided in Volume  II  of  the  New  Government  Accounting  System
(NGAS) to facilitate verification of the inventory report.

Recommendation

      We recommend that the Municipal Accountant:  1.)  Set-up  depreciation
expense for the corresponding years where depreciation  was  not  recognized
and the corresponding JEV of the same  to  effect  the  adjustments  in  the
ensuing year; and 2.) Maintain PPELC of  each  PPE  to  record  acquisition,
description, custody, estimated  useful  life,  depreciation,  disposal  and
other information about the PPE.

Recommendation:

      We recommend that share in the proceeds of the Community Tax and  Real
Property Tax due to the barangay be released within  the  prescribed  period
provided for under R.A. 7160.

Recommendation:

      We recommend that  management:  1.)  Strictly  follow  the  limitation
guidelines  in  appropriating  for  discretionary  fund  and   extraordinary
expenses as provided under Section 325 (h) of R.A. 7160 of two percent  (2%)
of the actual receipts derived from basic real  property  tax  in  the  next
preceding year; 2.) Increase the collection of  the  real  property  tax  in
order to increase appropriation of the discretionary fund; and 3.)  Utilized
the funds within the allocated budget or appropriation.

Recommendation:

      We recommend to management the following: 1.) Strictly comply with the
provisions  of  COA  Circular  No.  2009-001  dated  February  12,  2009  to
facilitate the review and evaluation process and attention is  also  invited
to the penalty clause provision of said Circular; and 2.) Submit  copies  of
the contract and  its  integral  documents  enumerated  in  Appendix  A  for
contract review.

Recommendation:

      We  recommend  the  following:   1.)  The   Municipal   Planning   and
Development Coordinator and the Municipal Development Council shall  monitor
the implementation of the projects as incorporated in the Annual  Investment
Plan; 2.) The Municipal  Engineer  shall  coordinate  with  the  departments
concerned to fast track the implementation of the  projects;  and   3.)  The
Local Chief Executive shall  ensure  compliance  with  the  responsibilities
provided for in COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.

Recommendation:

       We recommend that a printed copy of the Procurement Monitoring Report
be furnished this Office in order  for  us  to  verify  the  status  of  all
procurement process transactions as basis for our  audit  with  emphasis  on
the claim of honoraria of the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC).

Recommendation:

      We recommend to management the following:  1.) The  Local  Development
Council monitor the implementation of the projects as  incorporated  in  the
Annual Investment Plan; 2.)  The  Municipal  Engineer  coordinate  with  the
departments concerned to fast track the implementation of the projects;  3.)
The Municipal  Mayor  shall  ensure  compliance  with  the  responsibilities
provided for in paragraph 5.0 of DILG and DBM Joint Circular No.  2017-1  to
achieve desirable socio-economic  development  and  environmental  outcomes;
and 4.) Optimally utilized the 20% Development  Fund  allocated during the
year.

Recommendation

      We recommend to the management the following:  1.) Strictly follow the
JC No. 1, s. 2017 of  the  DepEd,  DBM  and  DILG  in  the  preparation  and
presentation of SEF budget using SEF Budget Preparation Form  No.  1  (Annex
A) and based it on the DepEd-approved SIP and DEDP in the implementation  of
ECCD Program, kindergarten, elementary and secondary, formal and  non-formal
education programs; 2.) Post the utilization report of  the  SEF  Budget  in
the website of the LGU and/or in  at  least  three  (3)  conspicuous  public
places for transparency and  accountability  in  compliance  with  the  Full
Disclosure Policy of the DILG; 3.) Furnish COA with copies of the  Quarterly
and Annual Reports of SEF Utilization, as prepared by the Local  Accountant,
received by DepEd, the local sanggunian concerned and  Regional  Offices  of
the DBM and DILG; and 4.) In the  preparation  of  the  Local  School  Board
Budget, the members of the Local School Board  give  priority  to  programs,
activities and projects based in the DepEd-approved SIP and DEDP  especially
in the implementation of the  ECCD  Program,  kindergarten,  elementary  and
secondary, formal and non-formal education programs  and  utilize  the  same
to such purpose prescribed by law for which the SEF was created.

Recommendation:

          We recommend to management the following: 1.) prepare  and  submit
the Monthly Report on Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and  Management
Fund (Annex B) for evaluation and verification within the prescribed  period
set; 2.) Use NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No.  2013-
1    as    a    guide    in    the    formulation    of     the     LDRRMFIP
programs/projects/activities using the four (4) thematic areas  as  mandated
in R.A. No. 10121; 3.) Review and update regularly the LDRRMFIP to  consider
current vulnerabilities that may arise; 4.) prepare the Local Disaster  Risk
Reduction Management Investment Plan(LDRRMIP) and submit to COA  the  Report
on  the Sources and Utilization of the DRRMF,  certified  by  the  Municipal
Accountant on or before the 15th day after  each  month,  pursuant  to  R.A.
10121 and its Implementing Rules and  Regulations  and  Sections  5.1.2  and
5.1.5 of COA Circular 2012-002  dated  September  12,  2012;  5.)  Implement
optimally the programs, projects and  activities  contained  in  the  LDRRMF
Investment Plan; and 6.) Recognized in the books the  unrecorded  amount  of
P5,076,000.00  for  Trust  Liabilities  –  Disaster   Risk   Reduction   and
Management Fund.

Recommendations

      We recommend that the GAD Focal Point  System:   1.)  Strictly  comply
with the provisions of Joint Memorandum Circular No.  2013-01  of  the  PCW-
DILG-DBM-NEDA and other applicable rulings relative  to  the  submission  of
the approved GAD Plan and Budget and  the  preparation  of  the  Annual  GAD
Accomplishment Reports to be approved by  the  different  offices  concerned
and expenditures should be limited to the amount approved in  the  GAD  Plan
and  Budget  to  ensure  that  all  planned  projects  and  activities   are
implemented; 2.) Require the Heads of the  implementing  offices  to  ensure
that GAD programs, projects and activities are  implemented  as  planned  to
attain the GAD objectives; 3.) Maximize the utilization  of  the  GAD  funds
through the implementation of GAD-related programs and projects on order  to
attain the objective for which the funds were provided; 4.) Require the  GAD
Focal Point System to: a. institutionalize the GAD  database  with adequate
and systematically gathered sex-disaggregated data which  will  provide  the
bases for gender analysis, planning, programming and policy formulation;  b.
undertake gender analysis to identify and  confirm  existing  gender  issues
and ensure that the programs, projects and activities are responsive to  the
said issues; and c. revisit Annex B of  PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA  Joint  Memorandum
Circular No. 2013-01 which identifies the data requirements  for  particular
gender issues; and  5.) Establish a GAD monitoring and evaluation system  as
required under Section 3.4 and 5.1  of  PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA  Joint  Memorandum
Circular No. 2013-01.

Recommendations

      We recommend that management fully comply with the role of the LGU  as
provided for under the Ecological Solid Waste  Management  Act  of  2000  to
have a systematic, comprehensive and ecological waste management program.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management shall issue another demand letter to  the
former Treasurer to settle his cash  accountabilities  based  on  accounting
records; withhold the payment of any money due to such  Accountable  Officer
pursuant  to  Section  37  of  PD  1445;  and/or  initiate  the  filing   of
appropriate charges to recover the funds  of  the  Municipal  Government  as
mandated in Section 444 of RA 7160.

We restate the previous recommendations  regarding  this  matter  that
Management:

            •  Demand  the   concerned   officials   and   employees   with
              unliquidated cash advances to effect immediate settlement  of
              cash advances;




            • Refrain from granting additional cash advances to Accountable
              Officer with previous unliquidated cash advances and withhold
              salaries or any amount due to  the  concerned  officials  and
              employees pursuant to the aforementioned  provisions  of  COA
              Circular  No.  97-002.  Should  important  travel  or   other
              expenditures are to be incurred, the  expenditures  shall  be
              reimbursed instead of advanced until such time that all prior
              cash advances are already liquidated; and




            • Ensure compliance with the documentary requirements, more  so
              with  regards  to  the  Certification   of   the   Accountant
              concerning the liquidation of previous cash advances.

We recommend that the Collecting Officers be directed to prepare their
RAAF and the Municipal Treasurer to consolidate  the  RAAF  and  submit  the
CRAAF and RAAF of all Collectors at the end of each month to the Audit  Team
through the Municipal Accounting Office.

We recommend that the  concerned  Revenue  Collector  be  directed  to
remit/turnover his collections to the Municipal Treasurer  for  deposit  to
the depository bank on a regular basis in accordance with Section 29 of  the
NGAS Manual. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to exercise the diligence  of  a
good father of a family in monitoring the  remittances  of  collections  and
deposits of the collecting officers. She  should  set  control  measures  to
avoid or minimize delayed remittance of  collections  by  requiring  Revenue
Collectors to remit first the  proceeds  of  all  Official  Receipts  issued
prior  to issuances of  new  booklet,  otherwise  she  (Municipal  Treasurer)
shall be jointly and solidarily held  liable  for  any  misappropriation  or
loss of government funds under her control.

We recommend that the Accountable Officer be directed  to  record  her
collections and deposits daily in the cashbooks  to  enable  them  reconcile
the cashbook balance with his cash on hand at the close of each day.

We recommend that  the  bond  of  the  Disbursing  Officer  should  be
increased to ensure recovery of government funds in case  there  are  losses
of government funds.

We recommend that Management shall require  the  Municipal  Accountant
and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the balances of the  Cash  in  bank
accounts and verify the nature of any reconciling item noted in the  process
as basis for the preparation of the Journal Entry Voucher  (JEV)  to  record
the adjustments in the books of accounts.

We recommend the following:


        1. The GSO/Property Officer maintain stock ledger  cards,  property
           ledger  cards,  and  other  subsidiary  records  and   reconcile
           accounting and property records periodically;




        2.  The  Inventory  Committee  shall  conduct   complete   physical
           inventory of all property and reconcile results with  accounting
           records and effect any adjustments in the books; and


        3.  The  GSO/Property  officer  shall  prepare  the  Inventory  and
           Inspection Report on all unserviceable properties that  will  be
           identified during  the  count  and  facilitate  their  immediate
           disposal  to  prevent  further  deterioration  and  decrease  in
           salvage  values.  The  Municipal  Accountant  shall  effect  the
           necessary adjustment in the PPE Account to reclassify  the  book
           value  of  unserviceable  properties  to  Other  Assets  account
           pending  disposal  and  close  the   corresponding   Accumulated
           Depreciation account.

We recommend that Management furnish the Audit Team with the copies of
Memorandum of Agreement (MOA)/Trust Agreement on all  funds  transferred  by
National Government Agencies (NGAs) as provided for in  Item  3.1.2  of  COA
Circular No. 2012-001 to evaluate whether the  funds  were  utilized  within
the specified purpose.

We recommend that the Department Heads be directed  to  prepare  their
PPMP to be consolidated by the BAC Secretariat into the APP.   Require  the
Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that the APP is consistent with the  duly
approved yearly budget.

We recommend that the BAC be required to prepare and submit  regularly
the Procurement Monitoring Report to the GPBB every end of each semester  as
required under Section 12.2 of the IRR of RA 9184.  A printed  copy  of  the
report shall be furnished to this Office to allow the audit team  to  verify
the status of procurement activities specified  in  the  Annual  Procurement
Plan as basis for auditor’s action, if any.

We recommend that Management strictly adhere to the provisions  of  RA
9184 on the attendance of observers in all stages of the bidding process  to
ensure the achievement of transparency in the procurement process.

We recommend that the Bids  and  Awards  Committee,  through  its  BAC
Secretariat observe the proper advertising and posting of the Invitation  to
Bid as prescribed in  the  Revised  IRR  of  R.A.  9184  to  encourage  more
participants in the bidding process  and  intensify  the  competition  among
them, which in turn, will ensure that the LGU will  get  the  best  possible
proposals, as to quality and cost, for the project sought  to  be  procured.
The Municipality should have a specific prominent and public place  reserved
for posting of  the  Invitation  to  Bid  such  as  bulletin  board  in  the
Municipal Hall.

We recommend that the Management shall submit copies  of  POs  together
with all the supporting documents pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor  shall  require  the  Inspection
Officer to immediately furnish the Office of  the  Auditor  a  copy  of  IAR
within 24 hours after acceptance of all deliveries  and/or  soon  after  the
inspection so that a timely inspection can be made by COA.

We recommend that Management furnish the  office  of  the  Audit  Team
copies of  purchase  orders  together  with  all  the  supporting  documents
(certified true copies) to the  COA  Office  within  the  period  prescribed
under COA Circular No. 2009-001 to avoid the aforementioned penalties.

We recommend that the Municipal  Mayor,  as  Head  of  the  Procuring
Entity, and the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC)  strictly  observe,  in  all
instances of the  procurement  process,  all  pertinent  provisions  of  the
Revised IRR of RA 9184  on  the  procurement  of  goods  and  infrastructure
projects in order that the most advantageous offer is obtained.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant shall  maintain  a  special
account  in  the  General  Fund  for  the  20%  Development  Fund.  Separate
financial statements such as Statement of Financial Position,  Statement  of
Financial Performance and Cash Flows should be  prepared  and  presented  as
part of the schedules of the General Fund Trial Balance.

We recommend that the Municipal Development Council shall identify and
prioritize  the  development  projects  that   are   surely   workable   and
implementable during the year. Ensure that the  20  %  Development  Fund  be
optimally utilized  in  the  implementation  of  the  priority  projects  as
planned to help attain  the  socio-economic  development  and  environmental
outcomes in an effective and efficient manner.

We  recommend  that  the  Accountant  shall  strictly  adhere  to  the
pertinent provisions  of  COA  Circular  No.  2012-002  on  the  accounting,
reporting and utilization of LDRRMF.


      Specifically, Management should require the Accountant to prepare  the
monthly Report on the Receipt and Utilization  of  Disaster  Risk  Reduction
and Management (DRRM) Fund and submit to the  COA  Auditor  thru  the  Local
Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (LDRRMO) every  15th  day  of
the month.

We recommend that Management should instruct the Municipal  Accountant
to transfer unexpended balance of MDRRMF to the Special  Trust  Fund  under
the account Trust Liability-LDRRM (code 438) as shown below,  except  those
balances appropriated for capital outlay:

|.                                                              |

|                       |LGU Books                              |
|Particulars            |General Fund    |Trust Fund            |
|1. Incurrence of       |Dr. Expense or  |                      |
|expenses               |Asset           |                      |
|charged to the LDRRMF  |Cr. Accounts    |                      |
|(current               |Payable         |                      |
|appropriations)        |or Cash         |                      |
|2. Transfer of         |Dr. Subsidy to  |Dr. Cash              |
|unexpended             |other           |Cr. Trust Liability — |
|Quick Response and     |Funds           |DRRMF (with subsidiary|
|Mitigation Funds — MOOE|Cr. Cash        |for                   |
|at                     |                |the source and year)  |
|the end of the year    |                |                      |

We recommend that the Municipality should fully utilize the GAD Budget
in order to recognize the fundamental equality of women and men  and ensure
responsive governance.

We recommend that the LGU should comply with the Rules and Regulations
of  Republic  Act  9003.  The  Municipality  should  adopt  a   systematic,
comprehensive and ecological solid waste  management  program  which  shall
ensure the protection of public health and environment.

We recommend that Management direct the Municipal Engineer to:


   1. Provide the COA at the beginning of the year, the list of the  ongoing
      projects/programs/activities  and  those  that  are  to  be  implemented.  The
      list shall include the project name; brief  description  of  the  PPA;
      contractor or supplier, mode of procurement; funding source;  cost  or
      approved budget;  project  duration  including start and completion
      dates; and location;

   2. Inform the  Supervising  Auditor  thru  Audit  Team  Leader  that  the
      appropriate project  signboards  and/or public  notices  are  already
      posted. See  to  it  that  all  the  Programs/Projects/Activities  are
      publicized in the prescribed form of tarpaulin signboards as  required
      by Section 2.2.3 of COA Circular No. 2013-004;


   3. Furnish COA pictures of the signboard; and


   4. Submit regularly the monthly monitoring reports to the Audit Team. The
      report should also categorize the projects as to its nature, status of
      completion, cost and funding source.

Direct the concerned  officials  to  submit  immediately  the  reports
required pursuant to DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2016-21.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Accountant should: Follow the Imprest System. Replenishments should be made equivalent to the total disbursements incurred; Refrain from journalizing the transactions individually and through the Petty Cash Fund. Instead, record only the transactions singly as soon as the replenishment is made; Avoid charging expenditures that are regularly paid in nature such as the mobile plans; and Observe strict compliance with the documentary requirements enumerated in 1.2.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has already used the Imprest System.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the following: The Municipal Treasurer/Property Officer shall maintain stock ledger cards, property ledger cards, and other subsidiary records and reconcile accounting and property records periodically; The Inventory Committee shall conduct complete physical inventory of all property and reconcile results with accounting records and effect any adjustments in the books; The Municipal Accountant shall provide depreciation on all depreciable assets, reclassify those properties identified to their proper categories in the PPE class, provide the agency auditor the program of work of the backfilling of market site for the proper reclassification of the same and rename the Firefighting Equipment and Accessories account to Disaster Response and Rescue Equipment pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated 12 September 2012; and The Municipal Treasurer/Property officer shall prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report on all unserviceable properties that will be identified during the count and facilitate their immediate disposal to prevent further deterioration and decrease in salvage values. The Municipal Accountant shall effect the necessary adjustment in the PPE Account to reclassify the book value of unserviceable properties to Other Assets account pending disposal and close the corresponding Accumulated Depreciation account.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant has recorded depreciation on all depreciable assets. Inventory of all property is ongoing.
observations.reason: Incomplete data on existing assets and physical inventory is still ongoing. Reiterated in 2016 Report.

observations.recommendation: For drugs and medicines perceived to be necessary and essential for specific cases and circumstances but are not listed in the PNDF; a system shall be put in place to allow the facilitated review of specific drugs and medicines for such special cases upon request. The request should mention the reasons why ascertain drug should be listed, procured, and reimbursed by PhilHealth. If such request has not been acted upon within forty five (45) calendar days, then the request is deemed approved only for that particular purpose stated and only for that specific transaction. Such approval shall be valid for a year or until decided with finality by the Secretary of Health. Further, submit to this Office the mention request or an order that the procured drugs are listed in the PNDF.
observations.managementAction: Submitted letter of explanation regarding the incorrect usage of item mentioned; correct name of the item based on the NDF is Anti-Human Papillomarvi rus vaccine instead of Anti-Cervical Cancer vaccine. Certification was already submitted to this office in compliance with our Audit Observation Memorandum.
observations.reason: The Municipal Health Officer explained that the Purchase Request (PR) was already approved during the procurement of the aforementioned medicines but promise to comply with the provisions of the PNDF in the succeeding procurement of drugs and medicines. Certification was already submitted to this office in compliance with our Audit Observation Memorandum.

observations.recommendation: The LGU should enforce the examination of books of accounts pursuant to Section 6A.10 of the Updated Municipal Revenue Code of the Municipal Government of Sominot. Also, the LGU should have a database on its business establishment for monitoring and other statistical data use in the future.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer promised to assign collectors who will examine the books of accounts to intensify collection of business taxes to various business establishments located in the Municipality.
observations.reason: The Management has not yet furnished the results of the examination of the books.

observations.recommendation: The Local Chief Executive, as Chairman of the Local School Board and School Supervisors as co-chairmen should implement the programs and activities as presented in the SEF Work and Financial Plan for the year so that the intended beneficiaries/school children can enjoy the benefits and their educational standards will be uplifted.
observations.managementAction: The Management admitted that there were project/programs that despite the availability of funds were not fully implemented, however they promised to inform the District Supervisor that for this year 2014, the projects/programs envisioned should be implemented not to deprive the children of the benefits derived therefrom.
observations.reason: Prioritization of PPAs for SEF shall be done based on the amount certified by the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: Require the Personnel Officer or Human Resource Management Officer to follow up the reconciliation of the records with GSIS together with the Accountant so that the amount reflected as receivable from GSIS can be adjusted to reflect the correct balance of the account.
observations.managementAction: The HRMO promised to comply with the recommendation.
